title: Comforting Japanese Manchego Shoyu Ramen
description: A comforting and flavorful Japanese ramen dish featuring Manchego cheese and a rich shoyu broth, made with Buddhist-temple-friendly ingredients. This velvety and grassy ramen is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Bring the shoyu broth to a boil in a large pot.
Add the diced braised pork belly to the pot and simmer for 10 minutes.
Cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
Grate the Manchego cheese and set aside.
Assemble the ramen by placing the cooked noodles in a bowl, then adding the hot shoyu broth and diced pork belly.
Top the ramen with grated Manchego cheese, chopped green onions, sesame seeds, and pickled ginger.
Garnish with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
